messages.content:
An internet service provider needs to deliver 5G connectivity services to a customer residing on the 30th floor of a tall building. To improve reception of the 5G signal at that height, the provider uses a technology that enables an antenna to reconfigure and deliver better signal range and bandwidth.

What is the name for this technology?


--platooning


--beamforming


--massive MIMO


--pixel streaming
The correct answer is beamforming.

Why does it take so long for each generation of wireless communication technology to become broadly available?


--Consumers are resistant to change and have little demand for greater bandwidth.


----Devices that support a new generation are typically not available until years after the network is fully rolled out.


--Network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support next generation hardware.


--Network infrastructure for the previous generation must be shut down before the rollout of the next generation.

All of the options mentioned can contribute to the delay in the broad availability of each generation of wireless communication technology. However, the option that best addresses the question is: Network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support next generation hardware. Upgrading the network infrastructure and cell towers to support the technology, as well as ensuring sufficient coverage and capacity, can take significant time and investment. Additionally, devices that support the new generation may not be readily available until some time after the network is fully rolled out.

A health care client is looking to create wearable devices that constantly monitor the health of critical patients, suggest medications, and alert a doctor in case of an emergency.

How do 5G and edge computing work together to make this possible?


--by turning off devices that are used less frequently


--by routing all signals through a single central server


--by storing patient information in a blockchain


--by transmitting critical information with minimal delay

The correct answer is: by transmitting critical information with minimal delay. 

5G and edge computing work together to enable real-time transmission of critical information with low latency (minimal delay). 5G provides high-speed, high-bandwidth wireless connectivity, while edge computing decentralizes processing and data storage closer to the source of data generation. By placing computing resources at the network edge, data can be processed and acted upon locally, reducing the time it takes for the information to reach its destination. This allows wearable devices to constantly monitor patients' health, suggest medications, and quickly alert doctors in case of an emergency.

What is Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing?


--the number of custom-built cell sites we have implemented


--the amount of funds we have invested in cutting-edge technology


--our market share in the fields of cell networks and computing


--our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies

The most important advantage of Accenture when it comes to 5G and edge computing is our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies. Accenture has a strong track record in helping organizations navigate and adopt emerging technologies, including 5G and edge computing. Our experience and expertise in working with disruptive technologies allow us to understand the challenges and opportunities that arise with the implementation and integration of these technologies into business operations.

What happens after the initial rollout of a new network generation?


--The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime.


--Development of the network is frozen until the rollout of the next network generation.


--All devices created to work with the previous network generation must upgrade or lose service.


--The previous network generation will be deactivated after a transition period of a few months.

The correct answer is: The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime. 

After the initial rollout of a new network generation, the development and enhancement of that network will continue. The technology and infrastructure will be further optimized, and new features and capabilities may be introduced. This ongoing development ensures that the network remains efficient, secure, and capable of meeting evolving user demands. It is not necessary for all devices created for the previous network generation to upgrade immediately or lose service, as backward compatibility is often maintained to ensure a smooth transition for users. The previous network generation is typically not deactivated abruptly but gradually phased out over time, allowing for a transition period during which both generations may coexist.

What is a recent innovation in edge computing enhanced by 5G?


--transferring computing resources between geographic regions


--connecting applications to a telecom company's central data center


----routing long-distance phone calls through a network of cell towers


--placing computing resources physically inside the network infrastructure

A recent innovation in edge computing enhanced by 5G is placing computing resources physically inside the network infrastructure. This involves deploying edge computing resources, such as servers and storage, at telecommunication facilities or cell towers. By doing so, computational tasks can be offloaded and processed closer to the source of data generation, reducing latency and improving overall performance. This integration of edge computing and 5G enables faster processing and response times for applications and services that rely on real-time data analysis and low latency communication.

A large offshore oil platform needs to transmit signals between different devices on the platform with high reliability and low latency. However, they are unable to cover the platform with their existing Wi-Fi connections.

What is the best solution for the platform in this scenario?


--building new 5G cell sites between the platform and the shore


--adding a satellite link to the existing Wi-Fi network


--setting up a private 5G network on the platform itself


--routing all their web traffic through the company headquarters

The best solution for the offshore oil platform in this scenario would be setting up a private 5G network on the platform itself. 

Since the existing Wi-Fi connections are not able to cover the entire platform, a private 5G network can provide the necessary coverage, reliability, and low latency for transmitting signals between different devices on the platform. By setting up their own 5G network, the platform can have control over the network infrastructure and optimize it according to their specific needs. This solution would enable seamless communication and data transmission across the entire platform, meeting the high reliability and low latency requirements.

A homeowner uses a smart assistant to set the house alarm, get package delivery updates, and set timers on the outdoor lights.

What are these functions an example of?


--blockchain


---internet of things


--massive MIMO


--virtualization

These functions are an example of the Internet of Things (IoT). The Internet of Things refers to the network of physical devices, vehicles, appliances, and other objects embedded with sensors, software, and connectivity, which enables them to connect and exchange data. In this scenario, the smart assistant and the various devices it controls (house alarm, package delivery updates, outdoor lights) are all part of the IoT ecosystem, allowing for remote control, automation, and information exchange.

Why are 5G mmWave signals more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees?


--Its signals are transmitted at higher frequencies.


----Its signals transmit less information than older signals.


----Its signals are transmitted at lower frequencies.


--It is designed to work only within a single building.

The correct answer is: Its signals are transmitted at higher frequencies. 

5G mmWave signals are more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees because they are transmitted at higher frequencies. Higher frequency signals have shorter wavelengths, and shorter wavelengths are more easily obstructed or absorbed by obstacles. These higher frequency signals have difficulty penetrating through solid objects, compared to lower frequency signals which tend to have better penetration capabilities. Therefore, 5G mmWave signals can be blocked or weakened when encountering physical barriers, leading to potential signal loss or degradation.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


--an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


--a theater wants to record performances for its website


--a clothing company wants to track customer preferences


--an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server

The situation that would benefit the most from using edge computing is when an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data. 

Edge computing involves processing and analyzing data closer to the source, rather than sending it to a centralized data center or cloud. In the case of an offshore oil rig, where there may be limited connectivity and latency can be an issue, implementing edge computing enables faster data processing and decision-making at the edge of the network, reducing the need for data transfer to a central server. This can lead to improved efficiency, real-time monitoring, and timely response in critical operations on the oil rig.

Edge computing is often referred to as a "topology."

What does this term describe?


--a synonym that can be used interchangeably with “technology“


--a set of programming languages used to develop an application


--where the different parts of an application are located


--how much processing power an application consumes

The term "topology" in the context of edge computing does not describe a set of programming languages or how much processing power an application consumes, as mentioned in the options. 

In edge computing, topology refers to the arrangement, structure, or configuration of edge nodes and their relationships within a network. It defines how the different nodes are interconnected and how data flows between them. Topology describes the physical or logical layout of the edge computing infrastructure, including the placement of edge devices, servers, and data processing elements at various points in the network. It determines the architecture and connectivity of the edge computing system, which is crucial for optimizing data processing, minimizing latency, and supporting distributed computing at the network's edge.

What does latency indicate?


--the amount of energy needed to establish a network connection


--the number of devices currently connected to the network


--the amount of lag time between an action and response


--the number of MIMO antennas used to connect a device

Latency indicates the amount of lag time or delay between an action and its corresponding response in a network. It measures the time it takes for data to travel from its source to its destination and back. Latency is typically measured in milliseconds (ms) and can be influenced by factors such as network congestion, distance, and processing time. Lower latency is desirable, especially in real-time applications, as it ensures quicker response times and a more seamless user experience.

An air purifier manufacturer is looking for ideas on using edge computing to enhance its products and services.

Which approach would make use of edge computing technologies?


--a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed


----a local support staff based in the customer's geographic location for more rapid responses


-an automated program that can interpret customer service requests and assign agents accordingly


--a scheduling feature that activates a customer's air purifier at a defined time each day

The approach that would make use of edge computing technologies in this scenario is a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed. 

By integrating edge computing into the air purifier, the device itself can perform real-time analysis and processing of the air quality data locally, without heavy reliance on cloud or centralized servers. This allows for faster response times and immediate alerts to the customer when air quality levels are detected to be problematic. The edge computing capabilities enable the air purifier to make localized decisions and take appropriate actions based on the data it collects, providing enhanced efficiency and responsiveness.

What is a benefit of 5G mmWave technology?


--It easily passes through obstacles such as walls and trees.


--It provides coverage to low-density rural and suburban areas.


--It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.


--It has low frequency, allowing it to transmit over longer ranges.

A benefit of 5G mmWave technology is that it can transmit a large amount of information with low latency. 

5G mmWave technology operates at high frequencies, which allows for a larger bandwidth and faster data transmission speeds. This enables the technology to support a massive amount of data traffic and facilitate applications that require high data throughput, such as high-definition streaming, virtual reality, and real-time communication. Additionally, the low latency of 5G mmWave technology allows for near-instantaneous response times, making it suitable for applications that require real-time interaction and minimal delay.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


--transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


--using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


--delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


--moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data

The underlying concept that edge computing is based on is moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data. 

Edge computing aims to bring computational power and data storage closer to where the data is generated or consumed, reducing the need for data to travel back and forth to centralized servers or the cloud. By placing computing resources at the network edge, closer to the source or user, data processing, analysis, and storage can occur locally, resulting in reduced latency, improved performance, and more efficient use of network bandwidth. This concept of decentralizing computational tasks and data storage is the foundation of edge computing.
